pi_items.source: Task specification - valid SLD style name format
setup_geoserver.sh lines 135-157: workspace 'ne' created via REST API for Natural Earth data
GeoServer Filter Function Reference documentation confirms 'area' is a real GeoServer geometric function for calculating geometry area in the coordinate system's units
Task specification - threshold value used in SLD rules for feature classification. In EPSG:4326 (degrees), this represents square degrees
CSS hex color specification - verified as valid 6-digit hex code for pure red color (FF=255 red, 00=0 green, 00=0 blue)
CSS hex color specification - verified as valid 6-digit hex code for gray color (AA=170 red, AA=170 green, AA=170 blue)
export_result.sh line 14: PNG image expected at this path, verified as valid file system path with appropriate extension

privileged_info_summary: The task creates an SLD style named 'area_classification' in the 'ne' workspace using GeoServer's verified 'area' function to classify Natural Earth country features. The area function is a real GeoServer geometric function that calculates geometry area in coordinate system units. The SLD applies two rules based on a threshold of 15.0 square degrees: features below threshold styled with red (#FF0000), and features at or above threshold styled with gray (#AAAAAA). Both color codes are valid hexadecimal values. The layer 'ne:ne_countries' is published from Natural Earth's real 110m countries dataset via PostGIS. An 800x400 PNG preview is generated and saved to /home/ga/area_map.png.
